page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz and includes 8 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU.

This instance includes 75 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering low-latency local storage directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the r5ad.large sits in the lower tier of modern cloud compute options. Both single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests, along with Geekbench and Passmark CPU evaluations, place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show read speeds of 22.2 GB/sec and write speeds of 12.61 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, which also rank in the bottom 10%. However, the Passmark CPU encryption test and memory score show slightly better relative positioning, landing in the bottom 25%. Redis SET operations achieve 1.26 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.62 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5ad.large offers a high memory density and local NVMe storage at a lower tier of compute performance, making it a budget-conscious option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high CPU throughput. It is suited for small-scale databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that benefit from local scratch space but have modest processing requirements.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating at 2.2 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates modest processing capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally ranking in the bottom 10% of performance, though encryption and memory scores reach the bottom 25%. With 8 GB of RAM per vCPU and dedicated local storage, the r5ad.large provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand high compute performance. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications requiring local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with modest CPU requirements.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining dedicated local NVMe SSD storage with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for budget-conscious, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Includes 75 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor
Features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core
Suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
